visits.chiefComplaint: High blood sugar and increased thirst
visits.summaryText: You visited the doctor about your blood sugar being too high. You have been feeling very thirsty and tired, and using the bathroom often for the past 3 weeks. Your HbA1c level is 9.2%, which is above the target. The doctor is increasing your Metformin dose and adding a new medicine called Empagliflozin. You need follow-up blood work in 4 weeks.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: I've been really thirsty and using the bathroom a lot.

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: How long has this been going on?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: About three weeks now. I also feel tired all the time.

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: Your HbA1c came back at 9.2%. We need to adjust your medication.

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: It's higher than we'd like. Our goal is under 7%. I'm going to increase your Metformin and start Empagliflozin.

visits.chiefComplaint: Routine diabetes and blood pressure check
visits.summaryText: This was a follow-up visit to check your diabetes and blood pressure. Your blood sugar has been mostly stable but your HbA1c was 7.8%, slightly above the goal. Your blood pressure was 138/88, which is a bit high. The doctor kept your current medications the same and asked you to monitor your blood pressure at home.

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: How have you been feeling since our last visit?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Pretty good, but my readings have been up and down.

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: Let's take a look at your numbers today.

visits.summaryText: You had your yearly check-up. Everything looked mostly fine. Your blood pressure was slightly elevated at 140/90. The doctor started you on Lisinopril for your blood pressure and ordered routine bloodwork including cholesterol and blood sugar tests.

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: Let's go through your annual physical. How have you been feeling overall?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Generally fine, maybe a little more tired than usual.

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: I noticed your blood pressure is on the high side. We should address that.

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: Not yet, but we want to keep it under control. I'll start you on a low dose of Lisinopril.
